hawkish_phrases: wage growth was still strong...unit labour costs had been growing at a record pace of 6.6%
too early to be confident that the task had been accomplished
services inflation remained strong...momentum in services inflation, while declining, remained strong
dovish_phrases: headline inflation falling to 2.4%, from 2.9% in October
increased confidence that inflation would be brought back towards the 2% target in 2025
all three elements of the reaction function were moving in the right direction
risks to economic growth remained tilted to the downside
measures of inflation momentum...had essentially been back at target in November
summary: The Governing Council unanimously held all three key interest rates unchanged, maintaining a sufficiently restrictive stance with increased confidence that inflation would return to target in 2025, while commencing a measured tapering of PEPP reinvestments starting mid-2024.
